source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Noble Globetrotter II Hurricane Ida Incident

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Mississippi Canyon Block 809

event.top_event: Loss of marine riser and LMRP during hurricane evasion
event.incident_type: structural damage, lost object, pollution event
actions_taken: Blind shear rams closed and locked
Riser pull operation initiated then stopped
Pollution reported to National Response Center
Vessel docked for damage assessment and repairs
event.summary: The Noble Globetrotter II Drillship was conducting Temporary Abandonment operations on well P012 in MC Block 809 when Hurricane Ida approached. Despite T-time calculations indicating insufficient time to safely secure the well and evacuate, the operator and contractor proceeded with operations. A crew change delayed the temporary abandonment operation by several hours. The drillship unlatched the LMRP and began pulling riser but encountered over-torqued bolts and hydraulic torque wrench breakdowns. At approximately 7:00 pm on August 28th, the Captain initiated Stop Work with 12 riser joints and LMRP still hanging from the rotary. The drillship attempted to evade the hurricane at speeds of 1-3.5 knots but was unsuccessful against Category 2+ hurricane force winds and high seas. The riser broke below the rotary, sending approximately 11 joints and the LMRP to the seafloor. The incident resulted in approximately 3,700 gallons of miscellaneous oil spilled, one loss-time injury, over $1 million in vessel and equipment damage, and 115 personnel exposed to extreme weather conditions.
recommendations: BSEE Office of Incident Investigations to work with United States Coast Guard District 8 Officer in Charge of Marine Inspection (OCMI) to draft a Safety Alert

hazards.name: Suspended marine riser and LMRP during vessel movement

threats.name: Crew change operation during critical well abandonment phase
Delayed riser pull operation due to equipment failures
Unsuccessful hurricane evasion with suspended riser
Inadequate communication of evacuation failure to BSEE

consequences.name: Loss of marine riser joints and LMRP to seafloor
Pollution event - 3,700 gallons of miscellaneous oil spilled
Personnel exposure to extreme weather conditions

supporting_text: The Operator and Contractor monitored storm forecasts waiting on notification of a direct path rather than following operational T-times already calculated relative to current well operations and potential Extreme Weather Event.

Noble's T-Time & Evacuation Report for August 27th 2021 at approximately 05:00 am showed 52 hours to safely secure the well and 12 hours to evacuate/evade... -49 hours Decision Time. This meant the operation was hours behind in the attempt to successfully evacuate/evade.
The Noble Globetrotter II continued well work throughout the night even though the T-time calculations put them within the potential cone of an extreme weather event
Inadequate Planning prevented the drill crew from pulling marine riser and LMRP in time for the Drillship to evade/evacuate Hurricane Ida.
The decision to crew change during the TA operation with weather approaching delayed an operation already hours behind schedule and resulted in a failed evacuation/evade attempt.
At approximately 11:34 am, a Shell Regulatory representative sent the BSEE New Orleans District an email request to TA the well.

controls.name: Crew change management during emergency operations

supporting_text: Despite current conditions and T-Time & Evacuation Reports showing potentially not enough time to secure and evacuate/evade, Shell and Noble supervisors made a decision to conduct a crew change with the first of 4 helicopters arriving at approximately 07:52 am.

During interviews, Noble employees mentioned the crew change delayed the TA operation for several hours. The drill crew on tour was allegedly working shorthanded with only one crane operator assigned to two cranes.
The delay, coupled with an already depleted drill crew on tour, was not noted in the IADC or Shell's Morning Reports.

controls.name: Hydraulic torque wrench maintenance and backup equipment

supporting_text: During interviews, a Noble employee relayed over-torqued bolts connecting marine riser joints and hydraulic torque wrench breakdowns further delayed the riser pull operation.
The Drill crew had to find parts for hydraulic torque wrenches and ended up bringing hydraulic wrenches from the aft drilling setback area and installing them on the main drill floor.

controls.name: Stop Work Authority and Ultimate Work Authority (UWA) decision
controls.barrier_role: emergency work stoppage and risk management
controls.lod_basis: operational safety procedure and vessel command authority

supporting_text: At approximately 7:00 p.m., the Drillship Captain, in his duty as Ultimate Work Authority (UWA), initiated Stop Work during the riser pull operation with 12 riser joints and LMRP still under the rotary.

According to Noble's Stop Work Requirements, Ultimate Work Authority (UWA): The Ultimate Work Authority (UWA) is the person in charge of the vessel/facility and is responsible for ensuring work stoppages occur in an orderly and safe manner.

controls.name: BSEE notification of evacuation failure and rig status
controls.barrier_role: regulatory communication and emergency response coordination
controls.lod_basis: regulatory requirement and emergency response procedure

supporting_text: A rig move notification was submitted in the BSEE eWell reporting system, but neither the operator nor contractor notified the BSEE New Orleans District or Hurricane COOP Team that the Drillship, which included 115 personnel onboard, would be unsuccessful in evacuating from severe Hurricane conditions.

controls.name: Blowout Preventer (BOP) functions and well securing
controls.lod_basis: regulatory requirement and well control procedure

supporting_text: The well is currently secured with an RTTS packer set and tested. The Sub-Sea Blow Out Preventer (SSBOP) was left on the well the Blind Sheer Rams (BSRs) closed and locked.
On August 22, 2021, the 18 3/4" BOP was latched to the Tubing Head Spool (THS) on the P012 wellhead. Pressure tests were completed, and all BOP functions were made from the driller's panel.

controls.name: Weather monitoring and forecast tracking
controls.lod_basis: operational procedure and weather service integration

supporting_text: Shell and Noble leadership, both on the Drillship and onshore, were monitoring tropical wave weather advisories by multiple weather service providers.
An afternoon communication to Noble personnel showed Noble leadership had a low confidence in the forecast.

The Operator and Contractor monitored storm forecasts waiting on notification of a direct path rather than following operational T-times already calculated relative to current well operations and potential Extreme Weather Event.

controls.name: Safe Zone pre-selection and update procedures

supporting_text: At approximately 06:34 am, the Drill crew unlatched the LMRP from the P012 well and drifted to the "Safe Zone". This "Safe Zone" was pre-selected as part of the drilling operation and not updated based on current weather reports and potential extreme weather conditions.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
